user.title: Benni's Toolbox
user.description: Pick which module to add to the toolbox. Modules marked "pending" exist in the repo but are not yet fully wired — you can still create an entry for them.

module_step.title: Module setup
module_step.description: Configure the module-specific basic settings. You can change further details after setup via "Configure".

data.name: Display name
data.apply_enabled: Actually drive the cover (otherwise decision sensors only)
data.startup_block_seconds: Block apply for … seconds after HA start

abort.missing_module: Config entry is missing its module identifier.
abort.unknown_module: This module is no longer registered.
abort.single_instance_allowed: Only one instance per module allowed.

tv.title: TV
tv.description: TV media_player provides source / app / title for TV and streaming detection. Active / power are plausibility only.

appletv.title: Apple TV
appletv.description: Apple TV media_player for streaming app and title detection.

ps5.title: PlayStation 5
ps5.description: PS5 player provides title and state. External PSN title sensor optional as fallback. Power for plausibility only.

switch.title: Nintendo Switch
switch.description: Dock power and optional ping for the handheld hypothesis. ping_on + dock_off = handheld_candidate (diagnostic only).

pc.title: PC
pc.description: PC activity and power for gaming / private-time detection.

denon.title: Denon
denon.description: AVR media_player provides source (e.g. "TV Audio") — the basis for denon_audio_path. Active / power are plausibility only.

homepods.title: HomePods
homepods.description: Group or preferred player for music / radio.

context.title: Context / global sources
context.description: Cross-context signals — day phase, activity, window open, door, call monitor.

tuning.title: Volumes & debounce
tuning.description: Fine-tuning for Benni Media Context. These values drive volume decisions and hysteresis.

sources.title: Triggers & sources
sources.description: Which HA entity decides which mode is active. Leave a field empty to disable that trigger.

profile.title: Target positions per mode
profile.description: When a trigger activates a mode, the cover moves to this position. 0 % = closed / down, 100 % = open / up.

data.open: Open — fallback when no other mode fires
data.sleep: Sleep — bio state = sleep or night phase
data.privacy: Privacy / Away — truly away + empty household (bei_eltern doesn't count)
data.glare_tv: TV glare — media context TV/movie at daytime
data.glare_pc: PC glare — gaming on PC at daytime
data.window_open: Window open — window-open trigger active (absolute override)

data.apply_enabled: Actually drive the cover (otherwise decision sensors only)
data.startup_block_seconds: Block apply for … seconds after HA start

device_basics.title: Device — basics
device_basics.description: Name, switch and device kind. Sensors and thresholds follow in the next steps.

device_sensors.title: Device — sensors

device_advanced.title: Device — thresholds & behaviour
device_advanced.description:
Preset: {preset}

Only fields relevant for this device kind are shown.

data.stable_off_seconds: Switch off only after stable-idle for seconds
data.allowed_contexts: Allowed day contexts (policy SC) — morning / day / evening / night; detailed phase values (e.g. late_morning, afternoon, early_night) are mapped onto these four buckets internally
